plan: Trim SPY to 20% to meet concentration limits and reduce overall risk.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure, aligning with capital preservation.
Increase QQQI to 25% and QQQ to 20% for stable income and broad market exposure.
Initiate a new position in GOOGL at 10%, a top-quality name from the scanner.
Maintain a cash buffer of ~15-20% by reducing overall market exposure.
This plan prioritizes capital preservation and steady, low-drawdown returns.
conservative.read: This conservative plan targets around 1.5% per month, focusing on capital preservation and stability. It significantly reduces leverage and concentration, but still carries market risk. Expected drawdowns are minimized, but growth potential is also capped.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet concentration limits and reduce gross leverage.
Sell SOXL entirely due to its downtrend and below-stop status.
Trim SPAL to 5% to maintain a small, controlled leveraged ETF exposure.
Reduce QQQI to 15% as it was flagged for trimming by the scanner.
Increase SMH to 15% as it is a strong sector ETF with a good scanner read.
Initiate a new position in GOOGL at 10%, a high-quality scanner setup.
balanced.read: This balanced plan aims for around 2.5% per month, prioritizing risk-first growth. It reduces overall leverage and concentration while blending broad market exposure with quality tech. While still exposed to market fluctuations, the risk is more controlled than the current book.

moves.note: Add to leveraged ETF within limits

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the single name concentration mandate.
Sell SOXL entirely as it is in a downtrend and below its stop, aligning with discipline.
Increase SPAL to 10%, utilizing leveraged ETFs within the mandate for aggressive growth.
Reduce QQQI to 10% as it was flagged for trimming by the scanner.
Initiate a new position in GOOGL at 15%, leveraging its high scanner score.
Buy NET at 10% for its strong scanner setup and high risk/reward.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets around 4% per month, stretching for growth by using leverage and concentrating in high-scoring setups. This approach significantly amplifies both potential upside and drawdown. Leveraged ETFs like SPAL decay daily and can lose a lot very fast; strict stop discipline is critical. Expect higher volatility and larger potential losses.

plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and free capital for income-focused assets.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure, aligning with income focus.
Increase QQQI to 30% to prioritize steady monthly distributions.
Reduce QQQ to 10% to shift focus from growth to income and stability.
Trim CRWD to 5% to reduce exposure to high-beta single names.
Maintain a cash buffer and prioritize low drawdown and reliable income over growth.
income.read: This income plan targets around 1% per month, focusing on parking cash for yield through income and covered-call ETFs. It cuts leverage and high-beta names to near zero, prioritizing low drawdown. Distributions are variable and NAV can still fall in a selloff, so this is not a savings account but aims for cash-like income.
